title: PromptSpec
description: Declarative specification for a PromptGenie prompt execution unit.

name.description: Human-readable name for this prompt spec.

description.description: Optional longer description of what this spec does.

target.description: PromptGenie target profile name (e.g. claude-code, chatgpt).

template.description: Template name from the template registry to use as the base prompt.

oneOf.description: Secret variable binding — value sourced from an environment variable.

from_env.description: Name of the environment variable to read the value from.

secret.description: Whether to redact this variable in logs and history.

default.description: Fallback value if the environment variable is not set.

vars.description: Inline variable values merged with --var flags and --vars files. Use {from_env: VAR_NAME, secret: true} for secret bindings.

context.description: Ordered list of context sources assembled before sending to provider.

policy.description: Policy file paths or built-in policy names applied as a pre-send gate.

model.description: Override the model name (e.g. claude-opus-4-5, gpt-4o, llama3).

system_prompt.description: System prompt injected before the assembled context+prompt.

output_contract.description: Expected output shape — used to validate the provider response.

run.description: Runtime options (timeout, retries, dry_run, stream, etc.).

label.description: Optional label shown in source manifest.

max_bytes.description: Truncate source to this many bytes.

policy_gated.description: Whether this source requires policy approval (url/cmd types).

format.description: Expected format of the provider response.

schema.description: JSON Schema the response must conform to (format: json only).

require_clean.description: Abort run if git working tree is dirty.

no_history.description: Do not persist this run to history.
